          Common Questions

          What is a monitored table?
          Datadog lets you sync as many tables as you want from your warehouse or database. These only count as monitored tables once you add a monitor (a quality check like freshness, nullness, or row count) to a table, view, materialized view, or column.
          Do I have to monitor every table in my warehouse?
          No, you can monitor as many or as few tables as you’d like. Most customers start with a critical subset, like business-facing tables and key dependencies, and expand coverage over time.
          Is there a minimum number of monitored tables or can I start with 1?
          There is no minimum, and you can start with one monitored table.
          What if the number of monitored tables fluctuates throughout the month?
          Datadog measures the number of monitored tables by hour, then charges based on the average number of monitored tables over the full month.
          What if I have a group by monitor on a table with 100 monitored groups?
          You are charged for one monitored table, even if a group-by monitor evaluates many groups on that table.
          Are lineage and metadata visibility billed separately?
          No, lineage, schema, and metadata syncing is included at no additional charge. You are only billed for monitored tables and any Jobs Monitoring usage.
          What if I connect more integrations?
          You can connect as many integrations as you’d like. You will only be charged for the number of tables you monitor, along with any charges from Jobs Monitoring.
          Does column-level lineage only apply for monitored tables?
          No, Datadog can sync all tables it has permission to access, so you can view column-level lineage even for tables you are not monitoring.
          What if I start monitoring jobs?
          When you enable Jobs Monitoring, it is billed separately from monitored tables based on the host hours or job hours Datadog observes. This usage is independent of how many data integrations you connect.
          Will monitoring impact my warehouse performance or costs?
          Datadog minimizes overhead by using system metadata instead of table scans whenever possible, and you can control which monitors run and how often they run to manage performance and cost impact.
          Is this available in all regions?
          Quality Monitoring is available in all our AP, EU, and US data centers. It is currently not available in GovCloud (US).

This unified approach simplifies code security across the entire Software Development Lifecycle, also incorporating capabilities for scanning source code repositories through its SAST and Static SCA components.
          How is the Code Security Bundle billed on-demand?
          The Code Security Bundle is only billed on-demand if you have overages on a contractual commitment, For example, if you have a commitment for 20 committers but usage reflects 25 committers, 5 extra committers will be billed at the on-demand bundle rate. If the Code Security Bundle is not on your contract, you will be billed the on-demand rate for each standalone product you have enabled. For example, if you don’t have the Code Security Bundle or any standalone product on your contract, but enable Static SCA, Static Code Analysis (SAST), Runtime Code Analysis (IAST), and Runtime SCA on-demand (all included in the bundle), you will be billed separately for each product at their respective on-demand rate.
          How does Datadog Runtime Code Analysis (IAST) work?
          Datadog Runtime Code Analysis (IAST) uses instrumentation to analyze applications in their running state, monitoring requests and their interactions with each internal layer—operating similarly to Application Performance Monitoring (APM). This instrumentation-based approach is called Interactive Application Security Testing (IAST), which identifies code-level vulnerabilities in applications based solely on application traffic, and does not require any scans or security tests, ensuring that vulnerabilities are identified and addressed as the application runs. Learn more about Datadog Runtime Code Analysis (IAST) in our documentation.
          How does Datadog Runtime SCA work?
          When an APM-instrumented service starts, the tracer collects the list of libraries that the application is actively loading and reports the list of vulnerabilities associated with those libraries. Learn more about Runtime SCA in our documentation.
          Are Static Code Analysis (SAST), Runtime Code Analysis (IAST) and Software Composition Analysis (Runtime and Static SCA) available in all regions?
          Yes, with the exception of the US1-Fed region.
